body:
Fixes the five UI/UX polish defects reported in #550 (mirrored upstream as
link-assistant/hive-mind#1963), plus the codebase-wide consistency requirements.

Closes #550.

A deep case study — full timeline, every requirement, per-problem root causes,
solution plans and library/Chakra research — is compiled in the tracking repo:
[`docs/case-studies/issue-1963/`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/tree/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963).

---

## What was wrong, and why

None of these is a fresh bug — each is a residual rough edge from features shipped
quickly across #488 (the thinking preview) and #541/#549 (the theme pass + desktop
auto-update panel). The thread that connects P4 and P5 is that `styles.css` uses
**zero CSS custom properties**: light and dark are *manually duplicated hex* across
three layers, and each interactive element is styled by hand — so any new surface
or button silently misses rules the older ones have.

| # | Symptom | Root cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | The reasoning fade repeats per step line, so two stacked steps show two separate gradients instead of one "more below" fade. | The `mask-image` gradient was on the per-line `.thinking-preview-previous`, not on the scrolling container. | Move the gradient to `.thinking-preview-collapsed:has(.thinking-preview-previous)` as a single `linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent 0, #000 1.4em)`; drop the per-line mask. `:has()` keeps a lone first step unmasked. |
| **2** | Thinking detail is clipped mid-sentence, reading as "some parts are omitted". | Detail was capped at **120 chars** in *two* parallel constants — `truncate_thinking_detail` (Rust) and `thinkingDetailText` (JS). | Raise the cap to **600** in both, kept in sync; a pathological over-cap detail still truncates with an ellipsis. |
| **3** | A message showing only thinking renders a narrow ~116px body, then jumps to full width when the answer streams in. | A leftover typing-indicator rule `.pending .message-body { width: 116px }` (+ a dead `.typing::after`) pinned the pending body. | Remove the fixed-width clamp so the pending body uses the same width as a settled body — no reflow. |
| **4** | The desktop **services** and **update** panels render light surfaces in dark mode (washed buttons, dim status text). | When those panels were added, only light rules were written; the dark counterparts were never hand-duplicated. | Add complete dark rules for every services/update surface in **both** `:root[data-theme="dark"]` **and** `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`. |
| **5** | Only some top-bar buttons react to hover — partial, inconsistent feedback. | Top-bar controls were styled piecemeal; newer buttons never got the older ones' `:hover` rules. | One shared hover + active-hover + `:focus-visible` ring for **every** top-bar control, light and dark. |

## "Fix it in all places"

Per the issue's codebase-wide requirement: problem **2** is fixed in **both**
runtimes (Rust core + JS worker, cross-referenced so they stay in sync); problems
**4** and **5** are fixed in **both** dark layers (explicit `data-theme` override +
`prefers-color-scheme` fallback).

## Chakra UI / JSX migration

The issue's strategic ask (migrate to chakra-ui.com + JSX, reuse shared components)
is **documented as a staged, multi-PR migration** in the case study's
[`solution-plans.md`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/solution-plans.md),
not executed here: the app currently ships raw `React.createElement` with committed
bundles guarded by a `git diff --exit-code` CI gate, so a JSX/Chakra transition is a
large infra change that should not ride with five cosmetic fixes. These fixes are
written token-ready (consolidated selectors, both theme layers) so the migration —
which starts with introducing CSS design tokens to remove the duplication root
cause — can absorb them cleanly.

---

## Before / after

### Light theme — problem 1 fade, problem 3 pending width, problem 5 controls
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| ![before light](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/assets/issue-1963-before-light.png?raw=true) | ![after light](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/assets/issue-1963-after-light.png?raw=true) |

### Dark theme — problem 4 services/update theming
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| ![before dark](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/assets/issue-1963-before-dark.png?raw=true) | ![after dark](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/assets/issue-1963-after-dark.png?raw=true) |

### Detail crops
| Problem 1 — one continuous fade | Problem 5 — shared hover |
|---|---|
| ![p1 crop](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/assets/issue-1963-after-p1-crop.png?raw=true) | ![p5 hover](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/assets/issue-1963-after-p5-hover.png?raw=true) |

*(Rendered from the production `styles.css` via `experiments/issue-1963-harness.html`.)*

---

## Tests

- **`tests/unit/issue_1963.rs`** (2 tests) — pins the new 600-char bound: realistic
  detail survives in full; pathological over-cap detail still truncates with an
  ellipsis.
- **`tests/e2e/tests/issue-1963.spec.js`** (10 Playwright tests) — behavioral
  regression for problems 1/3/4/5: a single continuous container mask, pending body
  width parity (≠116px, ≈settled width), dark services/update computed colors, and
  hover-background + focus-ring changes across every top-bar control. The CSS-bound
  tests fail (9/10) when the fix is stashed, proving they bind to the fix.

### Verification
- `cargo test --test unit` → **1021 passed, 0 failed** (incl. `issue_1963` and the
  `semantic_grounding` grounding scan).
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` → clean.
- `check:i18n`, `check:web-tdz`, `check:web-hardcoded-ui` → pass.
- `src/web/{vendor,ocr}.bundle.js` → unchanged (entry files untouched).

## Changelog
`changelog.d/20260621_133148_issue_550_ui_ux_polish.md` (`bump: patch`, five
`### Fixed` entries).

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/550

… services, topbar hover (#550)

Address all five UI/UX defects reported in link-assistant/formal-ai#550
(mirrored as link-assistant/hive-mind#1963):

- Problem 1: the collapsed reasoning preview faded each step line on its
  own, breaking the "more content below" illusion. Move the scroll-fade
  mask to the collapsed container so one continuous gradient spans every
  visible step; drop the per-line mask.
- Problem 2: naturalized thinking detail was clipped at 120 chars,
  truncating realistic single-step content mid-sentence. Raise the cap to
  600 in both truncate_thinking_detail (Rust) and the thinkingDetailText
  browser helper, kept in sync.
- Problem 3: a pending message that only shows thinking collapsed to a
  fixed 116px body, so the bubble jumped in width when the answer started.
  Remove the narrow clamp so the pending bubble keeps full message width.
- Problem 4: the desktop services + update panels had no dark-theme rules,
  rendering light-on-dark in dark mode. Add complete dark counterparts for
  every services/update surface, token input, action button and status line
  (in both the data-theme=dark block and the prefers-color-scheme media
  query).
- Problem 5: only some top-bar controls reacted to hover/focus. Give every
  control (source/download/report/memory, sidebar + mobile-menu toggles,
  mode/diagnostics toggles, mode options) one shared hover treatment and a
  consistent keyboard focus ring, in light and dark.

Tests:
- tests/unit/issue_1963.rs pins the 600-char thinking-detail bound (Rust).
- tests/e2e/tests/issue-1963.spec.js asserts the continuous P1 mask, the
  P3 width parity, the P4 dark surfaces, and the P5 hover/focus behavior.
- experiments/issue-1963-harness.html renders the verbatim DOM against the
  shipped stylesheet for manual before/after inspection.

Refs link-assistant/hive-mind#1963
Closes link-assistant/formal-ai#550
commits.messageHeadline: fix(web): polish reasoning fade, thinking detail, pending width, dark…

commits.messageBody:
The naturalized thinking-detail cap (problem 2 of link-assistant/formal-ai#550)
is language-facing, so its regression coverage must span every supported UI
language, not only English. Add Rust unit tests that assert a realistic,
sub-600-char detail survives verbatim in Russian (Cyrillic) and Hindi
(Devanagari), and that the cap counts Unicode scalar values rather than bytes
via a Chinese (Han) detail whose UTF-8 length exceeds 600 bytes while its char
count stays under the cap.

This satisfies the check:language-test-coverage CI gate, which requires that
language-facing changes (here src/web/app.js) add tests covering en, ru, hi
and zh.

Refs link-assistant/hive-mind#1963
Refs link-assistant/formal-ai#550
commits.messageHeadline: test(thinking): cover ru/hi/zh for the thinking-detail cap (problem 2)

title: fix(web): polish reasoning fade, thinking detail, pending width, dark services, topbar hover
